content.full_text:
COUNTY OF ORANGE

GENERAL SERVICES AGENCY

T. R. EGAN
DIRECTOR

SPECIAL SERVICES

MAR 19 1 50 PM '78

REGISTRATION & ELECTIONS DIVISION
A. E. OLSON, REGISTRAR OF VOTERS
1300 S. GRAND AVE.
SANTA ANA, CA 92705
(714) 834-2244

78-03-078

March 8, 1978


Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
Sacramento, California 95814

Gentlemen:

Enclosed is a letter from Rex Gaede regarding
campaign expenditures. In view of the fact that
Mr. Gaede must file his original statement with
the office of the Secretary of State, we are
forwarding his letter to your office for an
opinion.

We are mailing a copy of this letter to Mr. Gaede.

Very truly yours,

Registrar of Voters
Orange County


By [illegible] Shelton
Deputy

Enclosure

js

cc: Rex Gaede

Jeanette Shelton, Orange County Elections: Mr. Nelson (Rex Gaede's
(April 7, 1978) that
campaign consultant) was advised by phone line 1
campaign activities should
expenditures for congressional/state campaign activities should
be pro-rated with state expenditures reported pursuant to the
Act. (All expenditures had been previously reported to the FEC.)

=REX
★ REPUBLICAN FOR STATE ASSEMBLY
GAEDE 69TH DISTRICT

February 28, 1978


Mr. Al Olson
REGISTRATION AND ELECTIONS DIVISION
1300 South Grand Avenue
Santa Ana, CA 92705

Dear Mr. Olson:

As you know, I was a candidate for the Republican nomination in the
39th Congressional District prior to my recent decision to seek
election as State Assemblyman from the 69th Assembly District.

Accordingly, I filed all necessary reports and statements with the
Clerk of the House of Representatives, as required by Federal Law.
Since my decision this year to seek election to the Assembly I have
filed a Statement of Organization with the Secretary of State and
intend to file all required reports of contributions and expenditures
in connection with my current race.

During the time I sought the Congressional race I made two large
expenditures, the product of which will be utilized in my State
Assembly race: a management fee paid to my campaign consultant, and
a voter opinion survey of the 39th Congressional District, which
includes all of the 69th Assembly District within it. I will be
utilizing these services and the poll during the up-coming State
Assembly race.

Therefore, I wish to know if I am required to file any declarations
under State Law with regard to these expenditures. More specifically,
are these considered to be a contribution-in-kind to my State Assembly
campaign? If that is the case, how should I attribute a value to
these two "contributions"?

Your reply at an early date will be greatly appreciated.

Sincerely,

Rex Gaede, Mayor
City of Brea

RG/jb

cc: Fair Political Practices Commission

    Secretary of State, Elections Division


770 S.Brea Blvd.,Suite 220 Brea, California 92621

sections.question: During the time I sought the Congressional race I made two large expenditures, the product of which will be utilized in my State Assembly race: a management fee paid to my campaign consultant, and a voter opinion survey of the 39th Congressional District, which includes all of the 69th Assembly District within it. I will be utilizing these services and the poll during the up-coming State Assembly race. Therefore, I wish to know if I am required to file any declarations under State Law with regard to these expenditures. More specifically, are these considered to be a contribution-in-kind to my State Assembly campaign? If that is the case, how should I attribute a value to these two "contributions"?
sections.question_synthetic: Must a candidate report prior campaign expenditures (consultant fees and polling) from a congressional race that will be reused in a state assembly race as in-kind contributions, and if so, how should their value be determined?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request from a candidate to the FPPC (copied on the letter), not an FPPC response. The document includes a note from the Registrar of Voters indicating a phone call was made to Mr. Nelson about proration requirements, but this appears to be a separate informal communication and not the formal FPPC response. No formal CONCLUSION section is present in this document.

I will be utilizing these services and the poll during the up-coming State Assembly race. Therefore, I wish to know if I am required to file any declarations under State Law with regard to these expenditures. More specifically, are these considered to be a contribution-in-kind to my State Assembly campaign? If that is the case, how should I attribute a value to these two "contributions"?

embedding.summary: Rex Gaede, a candidate who switched from running for Congress to running for State Assembly, inquires whether he must report as in-kind contributions certain expenditures from his congressional campaign (consultant fees and polling) that he intends to reuse in his assembly campaign.
